entry.title: A recap of my amazing 4 stops in Brazil for the 3rd annual LATAM Roadshow 2026
entry.content:
As you may have seen we had an amazing 3rd SAP CodeJam LATAM roadshow organized for the third year by @ajmaradiaga and while I couldn't be there in March I made it down for not only some CodeJams but also an SAP Inside Track.  I got to meet with some amazing SAP Community Members, a few of our SAP Champions and some of my Community Relations colleagues.  Here is my stop by stop recap.  

Day 1 in #Brazil of my third annual LATAM roadshow for is completed (not counting travel days 😜 ) where I got to see some of the amazing sites in #Curitiba before heading to a meetup with a few colleagues, organizers, and an amazing sponsor: CHPro


The AI Core CodeJam with @thomas_jung and @RichHeilman  was sponsored by the amazing team at CHPro Solutions led by Calixto Herkert and our location host university Escola Politécnica PUCPR.

The pictures can’t capture the amazing energy and love from all the participants and their enjoyment going though the #CodeJam !
Many thanks to the volunteer photographer (I included some of her pics, abrigado!)

The organizers of the SAP Community Curitiba deserve all the credit, from @SimoneAzevedo   to @LuizSouza_cwb   and @AdrianoCWB .  Such a well organized event. I can’t wait to be back in Curitiba but now I’m off to #Campinas for an SAP Inside Track. SAP Community  is strong!

____

Stop number two of the LATAM roadshow for me was an amazing trip to a Campinas, a city just north to the north of São Paulo. The event is an SAP inside track (SAP Inside Track Campinas Inside Track Brasil 🇧🇷 just outside of Sao Paulo ) and I get to connect with my colleagues @antonia_zorn @noravonthenen and @Vitaliy-R  , as well as continue with @RichHeilman and @thomas_jung , but unfortunately for Rich and Thimas, this will be their last stop of the tour.

 

Also exciting is getting to meet so many community members, especially our SAP champions @RobertaFernande  , @MRobalinho , and @Marssel700 . Truly, there were so many volunteers and an amazing campus that hosted our event lots of great sponsors and familiar faces form the last two years. Kicking off today with a co-presentation with Antonia was fun and then watching my colleagues present, as well as so many other customers and partners from the SAP community. It was so many people learning and sharing about SAP  in the Brazil community, it’s just something that I cannot express enough, how vibrant the Volunteer SAP Community  is and the interest of the attendees. The food was amazing. The capybara‘s were amazing and I appreciated every conversation and photo, but I’m sure you could tell that….


Next, we got together for a meet up at an amazing local brewery. I don’t even think there was a way to count the number of people who came over and enjoyed time together socializing.


But like all good things this stop had to come to an end so me, Nora, Vitaliy and Antonia packed up and are off to our next stop Porto Alegre. See you there!
_____

My 3rd stop of the LATAM CodeJam tour was continuing with Vitaliy Rudnytskiy  , Nora von Thenen  and Antonia Zorn  to São Leopoldo/ Porto Alegre. This southern Brazil city and its people are amazing but wow the SAP Labs office is sooooo amazing.
 

 


We had two CodeJams and a meetup between Monday/Tuesday. A packed house both days. And a special shoutout to our local support who’ve been with me in Curitiba and will also join at my last sip of Recife. Vitor Ugo Fiori  Vitória Cristofolli.

 

 

 

 
The CodeJams were amazing and the attendees so great at participating and engaging both days. It was also great to attend a meetup at a local German themed fun event center with billiards and bowling. And I got to eat my first Xis thanks to Vitor’s guidance. I also got to trip in dramatic fashion in Parque Moinhos de Vento while out for a jog with Vitaliy, and thank goodness he was right there to pick me up as any good Developer Advocate would do!

 

 


On a side note, I also was lucky enough to meet some local SAP colleagues who before now I’ve only seen online. Thank you for your time and I wish we had more chances to meet as we are all SAP family.

 

p.s. When Antonia says “Did you see the colleague in the next room with blue hair?” Well I think you know what happened next, right Ângela Schmidt

__

And stop 4 for me of the #LATAM  #Brazil roadshow is in the books. It’s actually the end of the whole tour for the team in Brazil which is sad but was such a great time for the SAP Community . A special shoutout to Vitória Cristofolli  and Vitor Ugo Fiori  for their support.

Arriving from Porto Alegre to #Recife was such a dramatic change. A northern coastal town with such a different look and feel to the architecture and of course the ocean views

I did a quick tour of the downtown and actually got to see the first synagogue ever built in the Americas (Synagoga Kahal Zur Israel) which showed me so much personal history I never thought I’d experience and will share with my Rabbi.



Then it was back to work. Me, Nora von Thenen  , Vitaliy Rudnytskiy  and Antonia Zorn  were hosted by the amazing team at Accenture led by João Otto Gethmann Jr  .


The attendees were so warm and attentive and enjoyed learning from Witalij on day 1 and Nora on day 2. Me and Antonia shared about the community and also had the pleasure of seeing so many locals at our meetup on a rooftop.

Stories were shared, local food and drink consumed and code discussions all around.  SAP Community, #barbaAzul shall return…

entry.title: Re: A recap of my amazing 4 stops in Brazil for the 3rd annual LATAM Roadshow
entry.content: I’ll be honest .... I’ve been active in the SAP Community since 2007, and I’ve never seen a year as fantastic as this one for the SAP Community in Brazil. And we’re only in April. "Muitíssimo obrigado" to @josh_bentley “Barba Azul” @antonia_zorn and all the Advocates, and the local organizers in each city for making such a meaningful difference for SAP professionals from north to south across Brazil. See you soon "Até Breve"

entry.title: Re: 3XK In-House Repair – Spare Parts Consumption Timing...
entry.content:
Good day @Wei_Wum8 

Thank you for your question.

Yes, what you explained is largely expected for 3XK.

In the standard 3XK process, stock service parts are planned on the repair order. When the repair order is released, the system supports availability check and reservation for stock service parts. The actual goods movement for the consumed spare part is posted through the repair confirmation. In general repair confirmations are posted against repair order line items and that spares costs are posted automatically. Also, time sheet entries, goods issues, and expenses can be posted from the confirmation onto the repair order. So, this basically means that:

  • The spare part can be planned and reserved before the repair is executed.
  • The physical warehouse issue can happen operationally before the technician performs the repair.
  • The system posting of consumption is normally triggered by the repair confirmation.
  • The repair confirmation is the business step that records the actual consumed spare parts.
  • A separate standard “warehouse receipt by technician” or “parts handover confirmation” step is not provided in the lean 3XK flow.

 

So, to answer your specific questions:

Question 1: Is this the expected standard behaviour for 3XK?

Answer: As mentioned above, yes, it is. In the standard lean 3XK flow, spare parts are consumed through the repair confirmation. The process is designed around planning the repair, releasing the repair order, performing the repair, and confirming the actual resource consumed. That means, the service confirmation is therefore the key step where the spare part consumption becomes financially and logistically relevant.

Question 2: Is there any standard way to separate goods issue from service confirmation or add a receipt/confirmation step?

Answer: In the standard 3XK process, there is no separate standard step to post the goods issue independently from the repair confirmation from the same stock service part item. There is also not dedicated standard app step to confirm that the service team received the spare part before it is consumed. If your business needs this control, the practical options are for you to:

  • Use the reservation and availability check to support planning.
  • Use operational warehouse handling outside the repair confirmation step, for example physical picking or internal process control.
  • Use custom fields or custom logic to track a warehouse handover or availability status in the in-house repair process. KBA 3482680 confirms that there is currently no dedicated app to track the availability status of in-house repairs, but you can extend the Manage In-House Repairs app with a custom field and BAdI CRMS4_REPA_CUST_I_MODIFY.
  • If you require a more formal logistic execution separation is required, this will usually need to be evaluated as a fit to standard or as an extensibility requirement rather than a standard 3XK goods issue step.

Question 3: Does the reservation provide any priority or protection for service orders?

Answer: The reservation helps the system reflect a requirement from the stock service part. The system offers availability check and reservation functionality for stock service parts in the In-House repair process. However, do not take this as a full warehouse execution or physical stock handover confirmation. It supports availability planning and reservation of the quantity you required, but it does not replace picking, staging, or a separate confirmation that the technician has physically received the part.

Explaining the Process Steps for In-House Repairs

Whether other demands such as sales order can still consume the stock will depend on the ATP and its related availability check behaviour, the material setup, and the sequence of demands. Therefore, if your business requirement is strictly to protect your stock, you should test this specifically with a competing sales order or outbound demand in the same plant and storage location.

Here are references for more guidance

3580897 - Material Reservation Not Created from Service Orders - SAP for Me

https://learning.sap.com/courses/implementing-in-house-service-processes-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-service/explaining-additional-in-house-repair-functionality_ef11bc79-5f07-4f82-901e-7ee6ae805ec3

 

here is a quick guide:

open Manage In-House Repairs, create or open an in-house repair, add the repair object and confirm the in-house repair so that the repair object moves the next process step.

In Perform Prechecks, select the repair object, choose the follow-up decision to start repair and create the repair order.

Open Plan Repairs, search for the repair order, add a stock service part item, check the quantity, plant and item category and release the repair order. Check whether the reservation is created after you have released it.

Either in Display Stock, Monitor Material Coverage or Stock Overview app you have in your tenant. Search for the spare part material, check unrestricted stock before creatin the reservation, check the stock and requirement after the repair order is released.

Finally, open Perform Repairs again, select the repair order you assigned to the technician, choose Create Service Confirmation, enter the actual consumed spare part quantity and post the confirmation.

After you have posted the service confirmation:

  • compare the stock before confirmation,
  • reservation after repair order release,
  • stock after confirmation,
  • repair order status and,
  • material document or goods movement reference.

This will show that the system reserves or plans the spare part earlier but posts the actual goods movement when the repair confirmation is posted. The next step I would recommend is for you to test the same material with a competing sales order or other demand after the repair reservation is created. This will show whether the current ATP and reservation setup gives enough practical protection for your warehouse process. If your requirement Is a mandatory warehouse issue and technician receipt before repair, then the standard 3XK process will likely need an extensibility or process workaround rather than a configuration switch.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: WM Public Cloud Storage Type Authorization & Stock Co...
entry.content:
Hello Adel-Rihan,

I can give you some input regarding 1) Authorization Requirement (Storage Type Level).

 

My answers to your specific questions:

Is there any standard Public Cloud WM functionality to restrict user access at storage type level? No, “Storage Type” does not exist as a restriction.

Can this be controlled using business roles, restriction fields, or IAM configuration? You may set up business roles and their restriction fields based on a combination of different storage parameters like Plant, Storage Location, Shipping Point / Receiving Point, Warehouse Number/Warehouse Complex and Movement Types.

If not, what is the recommended workaround? The recommended workaround is restriction definition based on the above-mentioned parameters like Storage Location. Additionally, you may work with Movement Types as restrictions to define more precisely which processes can be performed.

 

You may use the Apps "IAM Information System" or "Display Restriction Types" to search for available restrictions. 

By using the available restrictions you can create an authorization concept according to your specific needs.

However, not every app / process checks restrictions in the exact same way. So after defining your business roles, it is necessary to run some checks to verify if your requirement is fulfilled.

 

Related resources:

  • Display Restriction Types | SAP Help Portal
  • The Movement Type Concept | SAP Help Portal

 

I hope this helps.

Best regards,

Kerisha

entry.title: Re: How to add new variants in the apps Manage Archiving...
entry.content:
Hello @li_zhang8ed3ad 

Thank you for your question.

In summary, the Add New Variant button is normally only available when the user has the correct authorization and when you can select an archiving object in the app.

Consult these references for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/73d60bd1893d489cb896bcec22a50ea5.html?locale=en-US

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/performing-advanced-configuration-and-role-management-in-sap-s4hana-cloud-public-edition-ehs-environment-management/developing-business-roles-using-templates

From your first screenshot, the left panel shows No matches found under Archiving Objects. This is important. If no archiving object is available to the user, the app has nothing to create a variant for, so the Add New Variant button will not appear. So, this basically means:

  • The issue is most likely to authorisation or scope.
  • The app itself may be visible but you (the user) may not have sufficient authorization to see or maintain archiving objects.
  • SAP_BR_DATA_PRIVACY_SPECIALIST is a business role template, not always an already-created business role in the customer tenant.
  • If you did not create a business role from this template, searching for it in Maintain Business Roles may not return no result.
  • You should manually create a customer business role from the delivered template or add the required ILM and data privacy business catalogs

Here are references for more guidance

3340679 - Changing the Batch Management Indicator of a material in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me

2986171 - Error message R111022 in the app Block Master Data - Business Partner - SAP for Me

2702011 - Deleting Employee and Contingent Worker Data in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me

To answer your questions:

Question 1: But in our system, the button [Add New Variants] is not showed.

Answer: As mentioned above the likely reason is that your user does not have the required business role, business catalog, or write authorization for archiving variant maintenance.

Question 2: Why the related business role [SAP_BR_DATA_PRIVACY_SPECIALIST] does not exist in our system, too?

Answer: The reason is the business role is a delivered role template, not necessarily an active busines role already created in your tenant. The common approach is to open Maintain Business Roles, choose Create from Template, select the relevant delivered template, and create your own customer business role from it. Only use templates mainly as a starting point and then tailor the productive role to your company’s authorization concept.

I suggest you check the following:

  • Whether the business role was created from template SAP_BR_DATA_PRIVACY_SPECIALIST.
  • Whether your user is assigned to that created business role.
  • Whether the business role contains the relevant ILM, archiving, and data privacy business catalogs.
  • Whether restrictions are maintained with sufficient write access
  • Whether the required scope item or archiving functionality is active in your tenant.
  • Whether the app library entry you checked is for the correct product version and release.

 Here is a quick guide:

In Maintain Business Roles, do not search for SAP_BR_DATA_PRIVACY_SPECIALIST,

choose from the Template, search for template.

Create a new customer role, for example Z_DATA_PRIVACY_SPECIALITS. Then save the role.

Go to Assigned Business Users, add your user, go to Assigned Business Catalogs and verify that the data privacy and ILM related catalogs are included. In Maintain Restrictions, check that read , write, and value help access are not restricted in a way that prevents maintaining archiving variant and save the role.

Log off and log on again after you have assigned the role. Open Managing Archiving Variants and check whether archiving objects are now displayed in the left panel. Select the archiving object and check whether the Add New Variant button appears.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Single billing document to be created with TAN & TAD...
entry.content:
Dear Sravan,

In this scenario, the requirement cannot be achieved through standard configuration alone without using a combination approach.

Business Scenario:

  • Sales Order contains two line items:
    • Item 10 → TAN (delivery-relevant)
    • Item 20 → TAD (not delivery-relevant)
  • During delivery creation, only Item 10 is normally processed.
  • However, the requirement is to include both items (TAN + TAD) in a single billing document created from delivery.

Standard Behavior:
In standard SAP, delivery-related billing (VTFL) only copies delivery-relevant items. Therefore, TAD (text item) will not be copied into billing via standard copy control.

Proposed Solution:

To meet the requirement, a custom item category approach is used:

  1. Create a Custom Item Category (e.g., ZTAD):
    • Configure in VOV7 with appropriate billing relevance (delivery-based).
    • Assign item category group LEIS to treat it as a service-type item.
  2. Item Category Determination (VOV4):
    Maintain combinations such as:
    • Sales Doc Type + NORM → Default: TAN, Manual: ZTAD
    • Sales Doc Type + LEIS → Default: ZTAD
  3. Delivery Item Category Configuration (0VLP):
    For ZTAD, deactivate:
    • Relevant for picking
    • Storage location required
    • Storage location determination

This ensures the item appears in delivery but does not participate in logistics execution.

  1. Assign Delivery Item Category (0184):
    Ensure ZTAD is properly mapped for delivery processing.

Copy Control Settings:

VTLA (Sales Order → Delivery):

  • Order Requirement: 001
  • Combination Requirement: 051
  • Header Data: 001

For both TAN and ZTAD at item level:

  • Order Requirement: 101
  • Item Data: 101
  • Business Data: 02
  • Update Document Flow: Active
  • Pos./Neg. Qty: +

VTFL (Delivery → Billing):

  • Copying Requirement (Header): 003
  • Copy Item Number: Active

For both TAN and ZTAD:

  • Copying Requirement: 004
  • Billing Quantity: G (Delivery quantity)
  • Pos./Neg. Qty: +
  • Pricing Type: G (Copy pricing from reference)

Expected Outcome:

  • TAN item is processed normally with inventory and COGS impact.
  • ZTAD item flows into delivery as a non-logistical item.
  • During billing, both TAN and ZTAD are picked from delivery into a single invoice, with ZTAD contributing only to pricing and revenue without inventory impact.

Regards,

Soumya Nath

entry.title: Re: How to allocate Capacity Units (CU) to SAC user licenses
entry.content:
Dear hiromi_nakamura79c62f,

Hope you are well. Thank you for your message.

To address this scenario effectively, it is recommended reviewing the following points to ensure a resolution:

1. If this SAC system is rewired to BDC as per Updating Your SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud Subscriptions to SAP Business Data Cloud | SAP Help Portal or directly created in BDC as per Provisioning SAP Analytics Cloud | SAP Help Portal,  it is recommended to first check blog How to use SAP Business Data Cloud Capacity Unit E... - SAP Community and How to use SAP Business Data Cloud Capacity Unit E... - SAP Community to have a more comprehensive understanding on how to estimate the capacity units for different SAC license types and how to distribute capacity unit across different applications. After the estimation, it is required to allocate capacity units for SAC in SAP For Me as per help document Editing Your Provisioned SAP Analytics Cloud Application.

2. If your SAC system operates as a Subscription-Based SAP-Managed System and has not been rewired or created in BDC, it is necessary to address any related license allocations or purchases from the perspective of your SAC contract. We recommend reaching out to the Account Owner or Quote Owner associated with your SAC contract to review and discuss any necessary license purchasing or allocation requirements. This approach ensures all necessary licensing aspects are appropriately managed. 

Thank you for your time.

Best wishes,

Lena

entry.title: ACDOCA_M_EXTRACT vs MBEW
entry.content:
Can we use the ACDOCA_M_EXTRACT to get the price of a product for a stock report.
We are using materials with
batch specific unit of measure (FERT),
with batch valuation (FERT),
without batch valuation (HALB, ROH),
With batch (FERT),
without batch (ROH),
project stock,
normal stock,
sales order stock.
Is it good to take the perunit price of a product from this report using the current period (999.9999 - FISCYEARPER). Or if its MBEW then how to consider all the stock table and scenarios to pick the stock

  • MARD: Storage Location Data for Material (current stock, e.g., LABST).
  • MCHB: Batch Stocks (Batch-managed materials).
  • MSPR/MSSQ: Project Stock / Total Project Stock
  • MSKA/MSSA: Sales Order Stock / Total Sales Order Stock
  • MSLB/MSSL: Special Stocks with Vendor / Total Special Stocks with Vendor
  • MSKU: Special Stocks with Customer (Consignment/Returnable Package)
  • MKOL: Special Stocks from Vendor (Consignment)
  • MSCA: Sales Orders on Hand with Vendor 
  • MSTB: Stock in Transit
  • MSTE: Stock in Transit Sales Order
  • MSTQ: Stock in Transit Project
Is it need to fetch values from all the belwo Valuation Tables ?
  • EBEW: Sales Order Stock Valuation
  • QBEW: Project Stock Valuation
  • OBEW: Valuated Stock with Subcontractor
  • MBEW: Material Valuation (Standard)

entry.title: Re: Problem reinstalling SAP ASE
entry.content:
@oneeyeman1

The trial license (aka EValutation license) can be updated/extended in 1-year intervals @ https://go.support.sap.com/minisap/#/minisap.  I have a pair of ASE installations that are a few years old and they run just fine on my (annually updated) EV license.

As for the express (XE) license, the license file has a generic key that should work on any host (ie, HOSTID=ANY).  In the past I've been able to copy the express license file (*XE.lic) to newer ASE installations and ASE successfully started up on said license (ie, the newer ASE software still recognized the XE license). [NOTE: It's been a couple years since I tested this workaround so I don't know if it still works with the latest ASE distributions.]

entry.title: What is SAP plant reallocation?
entry.content:
If you’ve worked in SAP transformation for long enough, you quickly realise that organisational structures rarely stay static for very long. Companies acquire new businesses. They divest underperforming ones. Manufacturing footprints change. Supply chains get redesigned. Tax structures evolve. Geopolitical events suddenly make one region less attractive than another.

Over the past few years, those changes have arguably accelerated even further as organisations respond to inflation, supply chain disruption, rising labour costs and geopolitical uncertainty. In many cases, businesses are being forced to rethink where they manufacture products, where they hold inventory and how their operational footprint is structured.

This often creates a very specific SAP challenge. A business may decide to move manufacturing from one country to another, consolidate multiple production facilities into one location or transfer responsibility for a plant from one legal entity to another. From a business perspective, this may seem like a relatively straightforward organisational change. From an SAP perspective, however, it can be significantly more complicated.

Enter the SAP plant reallocation project.

What is SAP plant reallocation?

At a high level, SAP plant reallocation refers to moving an existing plant from one company code to another, following some kind of organisational restructuring. While this sounds relatively simple on paper, the challenge is that plants sit at the centre of many critical SAP processes and are heavily embedded within both logistics and finance functions.

A plant is typically linked to material master records, inventory, production orders, purchase orders, sales orders, valuation records, warehouse processes and financial postings. Over time, that plant builds up a significant amount of transactional history and operational dependencies. This means organisations cannot simply change a configuration setting and move on. They need a way to move the plant without breaking operational processes, losing historical data or creating major disruption for the business.

Why do organisations need plant reallocation?

The reasons for undertaking plant reallocations vary. In some cases, organisations are moving manufacturing operations to lower-cost regions to improve profitability. In others, businesses are redesigning supply chains to reduce dependency on specific geographies after experiencing disruptions in recent years.

Mergers and acquisitions can also create scenarios where newly acquired manufacturing locations need to be aligned to existing legal structures. Similarly, divestitures may require certain plants to move into new legal entities while others remain with the original business. Tax, regulatory and customs changes can also force businesses to rethink how their operational footprint is structured.

Regardless of the reason, the SAP challenge tends to remain the same: how do you move a plant from one company code to another without causing unnecessary operational disruption?

Why is this difficult in SAP?

This is where things become technically challenging. Once a plant has been active for a long period of time, it becomes connected to a wide range of master and transactional data. This can include stock, open purchase orders, open production orders, batch records, warehouse management data, valuation records, financial postings and reporting structures. Organisations also need to think carefully about historical reporting, audit requirements and financial continuity. Even if a plant moves to a new legal entity, the business still needs access to historical transactions and reporting data.

The traditional approach

Typically, many organisations have handled this challenge using a manual approach. This often involves freezing activity in the original plant, creating a new plant under the new company code and then manually recreating the relevant master data and transactional objects. Inventory may need to be transferred manually, open transactions may need to be recreated and finance teams often need to perform significant reconciliation work to ensure reporting remains accurate.

While this approach can work, it is time-consuming, it introduces operational downtime and creates significant manual effort. It can also introduce unnecessary risk, particularly when large volumes of transactions need to be recreated manually. And from the perspective of the business user, it creates a break between the transactional activity in the new company and the historical activity in the previous company code.

The table-based transformation approach

A more modern approach involves transforming the underlying SAP data model at table level rather than manually rebuilding everything from scratch. Instead of creating a completely new plant and migrating operational activity manually, organisations can use landscape transformation tools to update the relevant SAP tables so that the existing plant reflects the new organisational structure.

This approach typically involves updating plant assignments, company code relationships, valuation records and other dependent logistics and finance objects. Because these objects are often deeply interconnected, this requires a structured and controlled transformation process. This is where specialist tools such as Natuvion Data Conversion Suite (DCS) are often used.

Landscape transformation technology enables organisations to analyse the impacted data objects, identify dependencies between tables and execute the required transformation in a controlled way, at scale and at pace. Rather than manually rebuilding operational processes, the software helps organisations transform existing structures while preserving transactional history and reducing manual effort. You might think of this as business redesign from the inside out.

This can significantly reduce downtime, minimise disruption to the business and allow organisations to adapt far more quickly when operational changes are required.

Final thoughts

Plant reallocation in SAP is not something organisations should underestimate. Even with specialist transformation tooling, careful planning is required to understand which data objects are impacted, how integrations may be affected and what historical information needs to remain accessible after the move. Testing is also critical to ensure operational processes continue functioning as expected once the transformation has been completed.

Plant reallocation may not be discussed as frequently as carve-outs, cloud moves or S/4HANA migrations, but I suspect it will become increasingly common as businesses continue adapting their operational footprint in response to changing market conditions. As organisations become more agile, their SAP landscapes need to become more flexible as well. The traditional manual approach can still work, but table-based transformation approaches are increasingly offering organisations a quicker and less disruptive way to respond to change.

If you would like to read more on this topic, you can do so here.

entry.title: ABAP Versions Explorer
entry.content:
There are several ways to check and compare code versions for SAP ABAP: the old classic method in the SAP GUI, a very good option in Eclipse, and the industry standard in AbapGit or on GitHub. These are all useful, but some activities are monotonous and take more time than desired.

 

Therefore, a new program Abap Versions Eplorer was developed with faster access to modified objects by program/include/FM, class, TR/TASK, or package, with multiple display and filtering options. An additional "Who is Blame" feature was also implemented to identify the actual authors of the changes.

Pick one of the available object types to view its versions. Input the name and press Enter. As this is a multi-window application, pressing Enter again will launch a new window. You can open as many windows as your monitor can handle without getting claustrophobic ).

There are several options for layout:

Side bar - by default, when data tables are on the left side, code is on the right side. It is good for Inline diff.

Or top-down layout - when code is below data tables and enough wide to show 2-pane diff.

We can hide tables in any layout by pressing toggle button "Maximize view" to explore code  and return back by pressing again toogle button "Standard View".

Almost all initial setting we can change by toogle buttons panel.

Show Diff - html diff view

Show Vers - usual GUI  code view for a single version.

Compact - show only changes

Full - show all lines + diff

The Blame function is useful for identifying the authors of changes.  Switched off by default as it is a bit time consuming.

By default we can compare double-clicked version with the previous one. But If we want to compare any version we should toogle Diff Pref to Diff any

choose a Base version and  press "Set Base" button,  then double-click any version to compare with the base one.

 

For example base version 16 was compared with v12.

So anyone can use/test this Version explorer. In case there is no AbapGit just copy and paste standalone version.

And as a bonus HTML/JS local comparer )

entry.title: Re: NLQ in the real world: what metadata setup made it successful for you?
entry.content:
Natural language queries have been something business people have dreamed about ever since computers have existed! But of course it turns out that it's WAY harder than anybody initially imaged.

It's fun to note that SQL itself was first promoted as an easy-to-use, business-friendly way of accessing data ("select customer_first_name from customer_table where id=12" is pretty easy, right?!).  

Since then, there have been many, many attempts over the years to bridge the gap between how people ask questions, and the complex code that is required to go and get that data. BusinessObjects Universes were one, of course -- the first "semantic layer" approach.

Straightforward questions work well -- if the metadata is up to date, helping avoid the problem of ambiguity and context -- different parts of the business use the same business term to mean different things. e.g. Headcount in an office means different things if you're talking about actual people right now in the building in case of fire vs. the total payroll. The latest products can spot this and use the user's profile to make assumptions, or ask follow up questions, ("did you mean...?").

But a trickier one is simple questions that require complicated processing to get the right answer -- e.g. "show me the top three salespeople in our most profitable branch office"' could require a cascade of calculations depending on how the system is set up. Now with AI having the ability to create "real unique code" on the fly, this is the next big barrier that might fall -- but so far, reliability is still a question mark.

And then there's the very real problem of uncertainty. There are lots of "simple" business questions where the answer is unknowable and/or requires extrapolation and judgement ("what will the revenue be next quarter?"). There are organizations that are trying to use the most advanced AI to gather lots of information (including external -- the economy, politics, storms, etc.) and come up with the kinds of models that human planners might put together.

entry.title: What is a SAP currency conversion?
entry.content:
If you read my previous article on SAP plant reallocation, you will have seen how political, economic and global change and uncertainty drive the requirement for complex technical change in SAP customers' ERP systems. 

Over the past few years, organisations have had to navigate inflation, volatile exchange rates, geopolitical instability and significant economic uncertainty. In some markets, businesses have faced sustained pressure from weakening local currencies. In others, governments have introduced entirely new currencies or announced plans to adopt new ones. Bulgaria’s recent adoption of the Euro is a good example of how these changes can quickly move from political discussion to operational reality.

When these events happen, businesses need to respond quickly. This often creates a very specific SAP challenge. How do you change the currency structure of an SAP system that may have been operating in the same currency for years?

At first glance, this may not sound particularly challenging. SAP already supports multiple currencies and most organisations regularly process transactions in foreign currencies. However, changing the company code currency is a very different challenge altogether. Once a company code has been operating for years, its currency becomes deeply embedded within financial processes, transactional records and reporting structures.

This is where a SAP currency conversion project comes in.

What is SAP currency conversion?

At a high level, SAP currency conversion refers to changing the local currency of a company code and converting the associated financial and transactional data so that the business can continue operating under a new currency structure.

This may happen because a country adopts a new national currency, because a business wants to move away from an unstable local currency or because organisational restructuring requires financial realignment across different currencies.

Regardless of the reason, the challenge tends to be the same. The business needs to move to a new currency structure. Your SAP ERP system, however, still contains years of financial history built around the old one. This means organisations cannot simply change a configuration setting and move on.

A company code currency is tied to general ledger balances, open receivables, open payables, asset accounting records, controlling objects and historical financial transactions. Over time, this creates a significant amount of dependency across the system. When the Eurozone countries moved to the Euro in 1999, the average SAP system would have been relatively small. Over the past quarter of a century, many have grown significantly. It's not unusual for SAP production systems to be 5TB and have billions of lines of transactional data. Some large ERP systems are ten times that size.

Why is this difficult in SAP?

If an organisation simply changes the currency setting at company code level, historical transactions remain stored in the original currency while new transactions begin posting in the new one. That quickly creates inconsistencies across financial reporting, reconciliation processes and audit requirements.

Businesses still need access to historical financial records and finance teams need confidence that reporting remains accurate before and after the conversion. Asset values need to remain consistent, open items need to be handled correctly and controlling structures need to continue functioning as expected. Integrations with downstream systems may also need to be reviewed.

Like many SAP transformation challenges, the configuration change itself is often relatively small. The real complexity sits within the underlying data.

The traditional approach

Historically, organisations often approached currency conversion through manual restructuring.

This might involve creating new company codes, migrating selected balances, manually adjusting financial records and maintaining historical environments for reporting purposes. Perhaps using application-level migration methods like BAPIs via a tool like LSMW,

While this approach can work, it often creates operational overheads. Finance teams may spend large amounts of time reconciling balances and manually validating financial outputs. And from the perspective of the business user, it creates a break between the transactional activity in the new company and the historical activity in the previous company code, requiring parallel reporting structures for at least a year or two.

The traditional approach often solves the immediate issue, but it is difficult and creates perhaps unnecessary complexity in the process. In some cases, organisations even delay the conversion because the perceived risk feels too high.

The table-based transformation approach

A more modern approach involves transforming the underlying SAP data model at table level rather than manually rebuilding financial structures from scratch.

Instead of creating entirely new company codes and manually recreating financial records, organisations can use specialist landscape transformation technology such as Natuvion DCS to convert the relevant SAP data directly. This allows the existing company code structure to remain in place while the underlying financial data is converted to reflect the new currency model.

This typically includes converting company code currency assignments, general ledger balances, open items, asset accounting data, controlling objects and related financial records. Because these data objects are heavily interconnected, this requires a highly structured approach that ensures consistency across all affected tables.

Landscape transformation software enables organisations to analyse impacted tables, identify dependencies and execute the conversion in a controlled way while preserving historical financial integrity. Rather than manually rebuilding financial structures, organisations can convert existing data with significantly less disruption to day-to-day operations.

This can reduce downtime, minimise reconciliation effort and allow organisations to respond much faster when economic conditions create urgent change.

Final thoughts

Currency conversion may not be discussed as frequently as other SAP project types eg S/4HANA migration, but it is becoming increasingly relevant as businesses navigate economic volatility, regulatory change and global uncertainty.

The challenge is that what initially sounds like a simple finance decision can quickly become a major SAP transformation project if approached incorrectly. Traditional methods can still work, but they are often slower, more manual and far more disruptive than organisations expect.

Table-based transformation approaches are giving businesses a far more efficient way to adapt when currency structures need to change, while preserving the financial integrity that organisations depend on.

If you would like to read more on this topic, you can do so here.

SAP Demand Driven (MRP) - Optimize Inventory Level and Secure Customer serviceSAP Demand-Driven MRP (DDMRP) is an advanced supply chain planning approach designed to optimize inventory levels while ensuring high customer service levels. It helps businesses manage material flow more efficiently by responding to real demand signals rather than relying solely on traditional forecast-based planning.Key Features & Benefits:Demand-Driven Buffer Management:Uses strategic decoupling points in the supply chain to buffer inventory where needed.Helps reduce lead times and prevent stockouts or excess inventory.Optimized Inventory Levels:Avoids the bullwhip effect by dynamically adjusting inventory positions.Reduces working capital while ensuring product availability.Improved Customer Service:Aligns production and supply with actual market demand.Ensures on-time delivery by maintaining optimal stock levels.Adaptive Planning & Execution:Incorporates real-time demand signals to drive production and replenishment.Uses color-coded buffer zones (green, yellow, red) for inventory control.

SAP S/4HANA - Working CapitalAP S/4HANA optimizes working capital by improving cash flow, inventory management, and accounts payable/receivable processes. Best Run Multi-CurrencySAP S/4HANA streamlines the management of exchange rates and multi-currency transactions, crucial in a global economy. It enables accurate reporting, seamless revaluation, and effective transaction management across currencies. This enhances financial transparency and minimizes risks from currency fluctuations. ​
SAP S/4HANA for Enterprise Contract ManagementSAP S/4HANA for Enterprise Contract Management provides a centralized contract repository integrated with ERP systems for real-time visibility. It enables easy search, tracking of contractual obligations, and proactive management of expiring agreements. This streamlines operations, improves compliance, and enhances contract oversight.
External TaxesSAP S/4HANA integrates with external tax engines to manage indirect tax compliance in multi-jurisdictional environments. This solution simplifies adapting to evolving tax laws as companies expand into new markets. It ensures accuracy and control, helping tax professionals stay compliant effortlessly.
Cash ManagementSAP S/4HANA Cash Management provides real-time financial data for accurate cash flow monitoring and decision-making. It allows users to identify and resolve issues, such as missing bank information, promptly. This enhances visibility and control over daily cash positions, improving financial efficiency.
The Continuous Financial CloseSAP S/4HANA streamlines the financial closing process by automating tasks, improving precision, and reducing compliance risks. SAP S/4HANA Goods Receipt and Invoice Receipt (GR/IR)SAP S/4HANA streamlines Goods Receipt and Invoice Receipt (GR/IR) reconciliation using machine learning and efficient processes. SAP S/4HANA's Financial Engine - Document SplittingSAP S/4HANA's Financial Engine introduces document splitting to balance financial statements at granular levels, such as segment or profit center. This ensures that debits and credits across GL accounts remain equal while meeting reporting requirements. The feature simplifies financial reporting and improves accuracy for organizations with complex structures
Verifying General EntriesThe SAP workflow for verifying general journal entries ensures accuracy before posting by involving specific roles. Requesters create and submit entries, while processors verify and approve them within the system. This process enhances control and accountability in financial data management. ​

SAP Digital AccessDigital Access Evaluation Service is one of various services provided by SAP's Global Adoption Insights & License Compliance (GLAC). With this free-of-charge service, SAP offers their customers support to determine the estimated initial number of digital access documents.Direct human access to the Digital Core systems (ERP & SAP S/4HANA) will continue to be licensed on the number of users, while according to SAP's Digital Access pricing model, all indirect access will be charged based on the number of documents associated to nine specific Document types.Based on a detailed evaluation of the relevant document types, customers will have a basis for the decision whether SAP Digital Access is the suitable license model for the future. The result can be used as a baseline for the Digital Access Adoption Program.

What Makes It Interesting

  • No upfront cost: Free trials lower the barrier to entry.
  • Enterprise relevance: Skills learned here are directly applicable to real-world corporate environments.
  • Cutting-edge tech: Access to AI, cloud, and advanced UI frameworks.
  • Community & support: SAP provides documentation, forums, and updates to help developers succeed.

entry.title: Create New Catalog, Space, Page, Section and Role – S4HANA Private Cloud
entry.content:
Like my previous article on classic concept of Fiori Groups in combination with Catalogs, this article is based on single-functional consultant approach where FICO consultants don't have to depend on ABAP or BASIS consultants to achieve overall objective.

We are going to develop a unified Fiori Space, create its relevant page, add related section therein, and then finally assign the respective Fiori tiles / apps.

For smoother learning and easier follow-up, I have devided this article in five main areas:

A. Create New Transport Request 

B. Create New Z Catalog and map it with required Fiori Apps

C. Create New Role and Assign the New Catalog

D. Create New Space, New Page and New Section

E. Assign Fiori Apps to Space, Pages and Sections

So lets start;

A. Create New Transport Request

Go to t-code SE10 and create a new TR where all configuration activities will be saved.

Click  button

Chose the radio button “Customizing Request”

New Transport Request will be created.

B. Create New Z Catalog and map it with required Fiori Apps

  1. In GUI, enter t-code /n/UI2/FLPD_CUST

Following window will open in the browser, this is SAP Fiori Launchpad Designer.

  1. We will create catalog for GL Report F2217 (Display Line Items in General Ledger)

Go to SAP online Fiori Library https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#

and find the app F2217

Go to Tab  and expand the node ‘Configuration’

Find the catalog (either technical or business),

I chose the technical catalog SAP_TC_FIN_ACC_COMMON

  1. Go back to FLP Designer page (shown in step B-1).

Click on button available on right top corner of FLP Designer screen.

Following pop-up window will appear, here select the Transport Request you created in step A.

Click OK button to close the pop-up window. Now all activities will save in this transport request.

  1. Click on button available on left bottom of the screen.

A pop-up window will appear, here provide the Title and ID of new catalog.

Click on save button to create our new catalog.

  1. Now in catalog search field, specify the standard technical catalog SAP_TC_FIN_ACC_COMMON

Here find the app F2217

Click on the button “Tiles” as shown below:

Tile details will appear in table as below:

Select the row and you will notice few buttons have been enabled in bottom tray.

One of these isclick it, and following pop-up window will show:

Here provide the new catalog ID which we created in step B-4,

Select the catalog and SAP will save the reference of desired tile F2217 in our new catalog.

  1. Now on same screen, select the button “Target Mapping”

Here select the appropriate Semantic Object “GLAccount” and Action “displayGLLineItemReportingView” as shown below:

Now click on buttonand following pop-up window will appear:

Here provide the new catalog ID which we created in step B-4,

Select the catalog and SAP will save the reference of Target Mapping in our new catalog.

Now specify the new Catalog ID in search field (as shown below):

Click on Tile and Target Mapping button to confirm the assignment of same semantic object and action.

This confirms the Tile mapping and Target mapping.

We have completed the creation of new Catalog and it’s mapping with the required app F2217.

C. Create New Role and Assign the New Catalog

  1. Go to t-code PFCG and create new role ZFI_GL_REPORTS by clicking the button

     

  2. Click on tab Select Transaction -> SAP Fiori Launchpad -> Launchpad Catalog

     

Following pop-up window will appear,

Here specify the created new catalog ZFI_GL_REPORTS to assign with the role.

Save the data and notice the new catalog assignment.

This completes the catalog assignment to the role.

D. Create New Space, New Page and New Section

  1. Create New Space

On Fiori, access the app “Manage Launchpad Spaces”

Click on button

Following pop-up window will appear, here enter the Space ID, Short description, Space title and specify the TR where space data will be saved.

Click onbutton

On following screen, as shown below, click on button.

Space is now created and saved.

  1. Create New Page

Access the Fiori app “Manage Launchpad Pages”

Following screen will show:

Here click on button.

Following pop-up window will appear, here enter the Page ID, Short description, Page title and specify the TR where space data will be saved.

Click onbutton to create the Page.

Following screen will show:

  1. Create New Section

On Page screen, click on  button.

Following field will appear:

Specify the Section title

Click on button to save the section.

E. Assign Fiori Apps to Space, Pages and Sections

  1. In GUI, go to t-code PFCG and select the role ZFI_GL_REPORTS, we already created in step C-1.

Click on button

Go to tab, and access the path as shown below:

On following pop-up window, assign the Space ZFI_GL_REPORTS

Click on  button

Now click on save button and Space will be assigned to the role.

  1. Assign Page to Space

In Fiori app “Manage Launchpad Spaces”, select the already created space ZFI_GL_REPORTS

Now click on  button

List of available pages will show.

Here find our page ZFI_GL_REPORTS

Select this page by clicking checkbox and then click on button

Click on button.

Page is now assigned to Space.

  1. Assign Fiori App / Tile to Section

Access the Fiori App “Manage Launchpad Pages”

Select the page ZFI_GL_REPORTS

Click on  button

Following screen will appear:

Select the Fiori app “Display Line Items in General Ledger”

and click on  button

Now this Fiori tile will show as assigned to page (as shown below)

Click on  button.

This completes the Fiori tile assignment to Page and section.

Now the required app is visible for user on Fiori interface.

Now you can see the tile under the Space and Page.

Just go to User Homepage and find the Space “GL Reports”

Here you can see the Page and Tile under the same Space.

This concludes the article to buidup the Space, Page, Section and Tile/App assignment.

Hope you have enjoyed the read.

Please do comment for any observations and recommendations.

Best Regards,

entry.title: Re: ABAP Versions Explorer
entry.content:
@yury_sichov   This is a very useful tool — especially for anyone who works across multiple transports, TOCs, or large development teams. The ability to quickly navigate versions, compare changes, and even identify authors through the “Who is Blame” feature is a big productivity boost. The multi‑window approach and the option to switch between inline and 2‑pane diff views also make real code review much easier than the classic SAP GUI versioning tools.

One thing I’m curious about as teams adopt this more widely: Have you noticed any performance considerations when exploring very large objects or classes with many includes and methods?

In some systems, version histories can be quite long, and I’m wondering if there are recommended limits, caching strategies, or settings that help keep the tool responsive.

Would love to hear your experience on this.

entry.title: Re: standard SAP report logic
entry.content:
I was able to sort this out, so sharing what worked for me.

In my case, I needed to reuse the logic from a standard SAP report, but I didn’t want to depend on the ALV layout because that changes during upgrades. The safest approach turned out to be a mix of both ideas:

Use SUBMIT only to reuse the core business logic,

but extract the internal table behind the ALV instead of relying on the ALV output.

Once I switched to reading the internal table directly, the whole thing became stable and upgrade‑safe.

Here’s the small piece of code that worked for me:

 

DATA: lr_data TYPE REF TO data.

"Capture ALV output
cl_salv_bs_runtime_info=>set(
display = abap_false
metadata = abap_false
data = abap_true ).

SUBMIT rsusr002 AND RETURN.

TRY.
cl_salv_bs_runtime_info=>get_data_ref( IMPORTING r_data = lr_data ).
ASSIGN lr_data->* TO <lt_result>.
CATCH cx_salv_bs_sc_runtime_info.
cl_salv_bs_runtime_info=>clear_all( ).
RETURN.
ENDTRY.

cl_salv_bs_runtime_info=>clear_all( ).

"Now <lt_result> contains the internal table from the standard report.

This way I reused SAP’s logic without rewriting it, but avoided tying my custom program to the ALV structure. For anything where SAP provides a CDS view or API, I now use those first — they’re cleaner and more stable.

Hope this helps someone running into the same question.

entry.title: Re: Req Object is not getting passed to bound action
entry.content:
The Fix: Normalize the Handler
The arguments you're seeing (entityDefinition, a, c, d, b) suggest the event is being emitted in a way that bypasses the standard action signature. To force it back to the req object, use an explicit arrow function in your init():

JavaScript
init() {
this.on('doSomethingA', 'A', req => this.doSomethingA(req));
// Use the arrow function to ensure 'req' is the first and only argument passed
this.on('doSomethingB', 'B', req => this.doSomethingB(req));
return super.init();
}

async doSomethingB(req) {
// Now req.params and req.subject will be available
const { ID } = req.params[0];
}

I feel this all happen because : -
Event Bubbling: If B is a composition of another entity, CAP sometimes emits "internal" events for the path navigation. Using the (req) => ... wrapper forces the handler to catch the actual OData request context.

Missing super.init(): Ensure you are returning super.init() at the end of your init() function, or the service lifecycle might behave inconsistently.

The URL Path: Ensure you are calling the action via the bound path: /B(ID=...)/doSomethingB. If the URL is slightly off, CAP might treat it as a generic call where the arguments get messy.

Try the arrow function approach first—it's the most reliable way to "reset" the argument signature to the standard req object.

entry.title: Create New Catalog, Space, Page, Section and Role – S4HANA Private Cloud
entry.content:
Like my previous article on classic concept of Fiori Groups in combination with Catalogs, this article is based on single-functional consultant approach where FICO consultants don't have to depend on ABAP or BASIS consultants to achieve overall objective.

We are going to develop a unified Fiori Space, create its relevant page, add related section therein, and then finally assign the respective Fiori tiles / apps.

For smoother learning and easier follow-up, I have devided this article in five main areas:

A. Create New Transport Request 

B. Create New Z Catalog and map it with required Fiori Apps

C. Create New Role and Assign the New Catalog

D. Create New Space, New Page and New Section

E. Assign Fiori Apps to Space, Pages and Sections

So lets start;

A. Create New Transport Request

Go to t-code SE10 and create a new TR where all configuration activities will be saved.

Click  button

Chose the radio button “Customizing Request”

New Transport Request will be created.

B. Create New Z Catalog and map it with required Fiori Apps

  1. In GUI, enter t-code /n/UI2/FLPD_CUST

Following window will open in the browser, this is SAP Fiori Launchpad Designer.

  1. We will create catalog for GL Report F2217 (Display Line Items in General Ledger)

Go to SAP online Fiori Library https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#

and find the app F2217

Go to Tab  and expand the node ‘Configuration’

Find the catalog (either technical or business),

I chose the technical catalog SAP_TC_FIN_ACC_COMMON

  1. Go back to FLP Designer page (shown in step B-1).

Click on button available on right top corner of FLP Designer screen.

Following pop-up window will appear, here select the Transport Request you created in step A.

Click OK button to close the pop-up window. Now all activities will save in this transport request.

  1. Click on button available on left bottom of the screen.

A pop-up window will appear, here provide the Title and ID of new catalog.

Click on save button to create our new catalog.

  1. Now in catalog search field, specify the standard technical catalog SAP_TC_FIN_ACC_COMMON

Here find the app F2217

Click on the button “Tiles” as shown below:

Tile details will appear in table as below:

Select the row and you will notice few buttons have been enabled in bottom tray.

One of these isclick it, and following pop-up window will show:

Here provide the new catalog ID which we created in step B-4,

Select the catalog and SAP will save the reference of desired tile F2217 in our new catalog.

  1. Now on same screen, select the button “Target Mapping”

Here select the appropriate Semantic Object “GLAccount” and Action “displayGLLineItemReportingView” as shown below:

Now click on buttonand following pop-up window will appear:

Here provide the new catalog ID which we created in step B-4,

Select the catalog and SAP will save the reference of Target Mapping in our new catalog.

Now specify the new Catalog ID in search field (as shown below):

Click on Tile and Target Mapping button to confirm the assignment of same semantic object and action.

This confirms the Tile mapping and Target mapping.

We have completed the creation of new Catalog and it’s mapping with the required app F2217.

C. Create New Role and Assign the New Catalog

  1. Go to t-code PFCG and create new role ZFI_GL_REPORTS by clicking the button

     

  2. Click on tab Select Transaction -> SAP Fiori Launchpad -> Launchpad Catalog

     

Following pop-up window will appear,

Here specify the created new catalog ZFI_GL_REPORTS to assign with the role.

Save the data and notice the new catalog assignment.

This completes the catalog assignment to the role.

D. Create New Space, New Page and New Section

  1. Create New Space

On Fiori, access the app “Manage Launchpad Spaces”

Click on button

Following pop-up window will appear, here enter the Space ID, Short description, Space title and specify the TR where space data will be saved.

Click onbutton

On following screen, as shown below, click on button.

Space is now created and saved.

  1. Create New Page

Access the Fiori app “Manage Launchpad Pages”

Following screen will show:

Here click on button.

Following pop-up window will appear, here enter the Page ID, Short description, Page title and specify the TR where space data will be saved.

Click onbutton to create the Page.

Following screen will show:

  1. Create New Section

On Page screen, click on  button.

Following field will appear:

Specify the Section title

Click on button to save the section.

E. Assign Fiori Apps to Space, Pages and Sections

  1. In GUI, go to t-code PFCG and select the role ZFI_GL_REPORTS, we already created in step C-1.

Click on button

Go to tab, and access the path as shown below:

On following pop-up window, assign the Space ZFI_GL_REPORTS

Click on  button

Now click on save button and Space will be assigned to the role.

  1. Assign Page to Space

In Fiori app “Manage Launchpad Spaces”, select the already created space ZFI_GL_REPORTS

Now click on  button

List of available pages will show.

Here find our page ZFI_GL_REPORTS

Select this page by clicking checkbox and then click on button

Click on button.

Page is now assigned to Space.

  1. Assign Fiori App / Tile to Section

Access the Fiori App “Manage Launchpad Pages”

Select the page ZFI_GL_REPORTS

Click on  button

Following screen will appear:

Select the Fiori app “Display Line Items in General Ledger”

and click on  button

Now this Fiori tile will show as assigned to page (as shown below)

Click on  button.

This completes the Fiori tile assignment to Page and section.

Now the required app is visible for user on Fiori interface.

Now you can see the tile under the Space and Page.

Just go to User Homepage and find the Space “GL Reports”

Here you can see the Page and Tile under the same Space.

This concludes the article to buidup the Space, Page, Section and Tile/App assignment.

Hope you have enjoyed the read.

Please do comment for any observations and recommendations.

Best Regards,

entry.title: Re: ABAP Versions Explorer
entry.content: Added: Per-instance cache for rendered diff HTML. Key: object type + name + versno old/new + flags (blame, two_pane, compact, debug). On hit — returns stored HTML immediately, skipping source load, diff and blame computation. On miss — computes as usual and stores the result. Cache is invalidated automatically when the popup window closes.

entry.title: Re: Asset data migration inconsistent
entry.content:
Hi 

 

The discrepancy you are seeing moving from a flat 2  (9,975.96 EUR) to 2.05  (10,207.95 EUR) is a classic behavior in SAP S/4HANA Asset Accounting during mid-year migrations. The system is likely not ignoring your 2 %  configuration rather, it is performing a Catch Up or Smoothing calculation based on the remaining net book value and the remaining useful life.

The Remaining Life Calculation Logic In S/4HANA, when you migrate an asset mid-year, the system validates the Net Book Value  against the Remaining Useful Life. Total Life: 50 Years. Expired Life at Transfer 28 Years, 6 Months. Remaining Useful Life: 21 Years, 6 Months or 21.5 years. If the system is set to calculate depreciation based on the remaining life and the remaining value, the math looks like this

Annual Depreciation = NBV at Transfer\Remaining Life

Annual Depreciation = 219,471.01  - 4,987.98  \ 21.0

However, if we look at your specific calculation  219,471.01 \ 21.5  = 10,207.95

This matches your "incorrect" value exactly. The system is taking the NBV as of the start of the year and dividing it by the remaining life as of that same date to ensure the asset fully depreciates to zero by the end of the 50-year term.

The National Code 2015 2% is likely a Straight Line from APC key. However, when you migrated the asset, the accumulated depreciation you entered 279,326.89 might not perfectly align with a mathematical 2% per year since 1998.

If there is even a small rounding difference in the legacy accumulated depreciation, SAP corrects the rate for the remaining periods to ensure the asset reaches a zero book value exactly at the end of year 50.

To bring this back to the expected 9,975.96 check  in Transaction AFAMP Change Period Control Methods and AFAMA Depreciation Keys, Depreciation Calculation Method, Check if the Multi-Level Method is set to calculate based on Remaining Life instead of Installation Date.  If it is set to Remaining Life, SAP will always adjust the percentage to fit the remaining value. Smoothing vs. Catch-up: In the Company Code settings for Asset Accounting, check if Smoothing is active. PT04 is custom right.

entry.title: Re: Effortlessly Sharing Themes Across Your SAP BTP Cloud Foundry Environments
entry.content:
Hi @andreas-roessler ,

I can't agree with the "well-explaine" in the documentation. I just had to setup SAP Content Agent Service and unfortunately it's a lot of try and error. I haven't found any mentioining of the Content Type that needs to be configured in the Transport Node. The correct one is "Multi-Target Application". Also I would suggest that you re-draw the picture in this post and increase the Subaccount box also arround Theming and CAS. The CAS Instance must be subscribed in the Subaccount where the Theme Designer is used. And the same needs to be applied for the Target Landscape where not only CAS needs to be subscribed but also an instance of the CAS with the Plan: application must be created. The service Key of this instance must be used to create the destination in the cTMS Subaccount.

Best regards,
Gregor

entry.title: Product Compliance / About content and what you may be expect to get
entry.content:
Introduction

We can see many Blogs regarding "Content" in Product Compliance.

First: we need to define: is there a difference regarding "Content" in Product Compliance compared to SAP EHS classic?

The answer is: partielly there is a difference. 

First you need to understand: what content comes via SAP and what content not.

And we need to define the "content".

In old days using 3E as an example content can be defined as:

  • you are getting new phrases or new phrase translations
  • you are getting new rule sets or updates of rule sets
  • you get new WWI templates and updates of WWI templates
  • you get new legal contetent or updates on legal content (including e.g. Dangerous goods content)
  • you get an update of property trees

Using this definition of content: this is now different (based on best knowledge)

On long term you are getting updates of compliance requirement and/or legal content via SAP.  This can include: new phrases and new translations of phrases and include new "CAS" numbers and update of legal content for the "CAS" numbers.

What is the same.. SAP (best knowledge) will not deliver "rulesets"  or "WWI" like templates. Here you are free to select a content provider.

What will be as well different: User Experience is different... And this can help to manage the content.

SAP plans do delivery "AI" and agents so what data maintenance can be supported. This is a new functionality not available wirh EHS classic.  What exactly we will get.. we need to wait. But this is a kind of new functionality and to a certain extent you might call this a new content. 

But we need to wait at laetss for on premise version 2027 (or a clod version) to see; what is delivered, how flexible this might be and to whcih extent the new content will help.

Links

https://community.sap.com/t5/product-lifecycle-management-blog-posts-by-sap/agentic-ai-at-the-2026-sap-ehs-and-product-compliance-conference/ba-p/14368872

entry.title: Re: im ERM wird die EKG in den Banf-Belegen nicht richtig...
entry.content:
Hallo @Markus_Boehm46 ,

mein Freund, dieses Problem tritt üblicherweise auf, wenn die Einkaufsgruppe in den zugrunde liegenden SAP-Tabellen (Bestellanforderungskopf/-position) fehlt oder inkonsistent ist, oder wenn die Extraktionslogik auf Ihrer Übersichtsseite das falsche Feld ausliest oder auf unvollständige Stammdaten stößt. Die Ursache liegt fast immer in unvollständigen Bestellanforderungspositionsdaten, einer fehlerhaften Konfiguration oder doppelten/falschen Einträgen in Mapping-Tabellen wie MMPUR_V_MG2PGRP.

Um die Fehlerursache zu finden, können Sie Folgendes überprüfen:

1. Prüfen Sie, ob die Bestellanforderungsposition tatsächlich eine Einkaufsgruppe enthält. Prüfen Sie die Tabelle EBAN, Feld EKGRP, für die betroffenen Bestellanforderungen. Wenn das Feld leer ist, kann die Extraktionslogik keine Daten anzeigen.

2. Prüfen Sie die Konfiguration der Einkaufsgruppenableitung. Insbesondere wenn Ihr System eine Materialgruppen-zu-Einkaufsgruppen-Zuordnung verwendet, prüfen Sie die Tabelle MMPUR_V_MG2PGRP. Entfernen Sie doppelte oder falsche Einträge. Stellen Sie sicher, dass die korrekte Materialgruppe der korrekten Einkaufsgruppe zugeordnet ist.

(SAP-Benutzer bestätigten dies als häufigste Ursache.)

3. Prüfen Sie, ob die Bestellanforderung aus einem Vertrag oder einer Bezugsquelle erstellt wurde. SAP überträgt die Einkaufsgruppe nicht automatisch von Verträgen in Bestellanforderungen.
Dies ist Standardverhalten.

4. Prüfen Sie das Layout Ihrer Dokumentübersicht. Stellen Sie sicher, dass die Spalte „Beleg mit Einkaufsgruppe“ tatsächlich den Wert EKGRP aus der Bestellanforderungsposition enthält und nicht etwa ein Kopfzeilenfeld oder ein benutzerdefiniertes CDS-Feld, das möglicherweise leer ist. Das SAP-Hilfeportal bestätigt, dass die angezeigten Felder von der ausgewählten Variante abhängen.

5. Prüfen Sie die Stammdaten: Ist im Materialstamm eine Einkaufsgruppe hinterlegt? Ist im Lieferantenstamm eine Einkaufsgruppe hinterlegt? Lässt sich im Belegtyp eine Einkaufsgruppe zulassen oder ist diese standardmäßig hinterlegt?

Hoffentlich lässt sich das Problem mit einer der oben genannten Ursachen beheben. Wenn Ihnen meine Lösung zusagt, akzeptieren Sie sie bitte.

entry.title: Re: I am getting a warning message while opening a work o...
entry.content:
Hi @sankets1 ,
My friend, you are seeing the warning because SAP is detecting the deletion flag on that specific material in the context of this order, while the other orders/components do not trigger the same check due to different master‑data or order‑data conditions. This behavior is consistent with SAP’s standard logic for message IM049 – “Assembly & is flagged for deletion”

The reason of warning could be the following as mentioned below.
1. The material is flagged for deletion (client‑level, plant‑level, or storage‑location level).
2. The material is used as an assembly/component in the order.
3. The system performs a deletion‑flag check for that specific order type/status.

From the SAP documentation on IM049, the message is triggered when an assembly or component is flagged for deletion in the material master and is still being used in a transaction such as a work order

The material is maintained as a component in the same order. The deletion flag is set at client level. Other orders with similar components do not show the warning.

My suggestion, SAP is treating this specific component as an “assembly” in this order, while in other orders it is treated as a normal component.
This can happen if, when the component has a different BOM usage or special procurement key. The order type triggers assembly processing logic. The component has Make‑to‑Order (MTO) or collective order settings. There is an inconsistency in the order header or component assignment.
